question: Jonah was the son of _______ (Jn 1:1)
options: Shammai
Azariah
Amittai
Ethan

question: What city did God command Jonah to go to (Jn 1:2)?
options: Babylon
Nineveh
Jerusalem
Damascus

question: Why did God send Jonah to Nineveh (Jn 1:2)?
options: Because of all their gods
Because they had slaughtered the people of Ephraim
Because of the oppression of all nations
Because its wickedness had come up before God

question: Where did Jonah flee to in order to avoid the call of God (Jn 1:3)?
options: Caphtor
Tarshish
Arabia
Tanis

question: What did the Lord do after Jonah boarded a ship to flee to Tarshish (Jn 1:4)?
options: The Lord sent a tornado
A rock punctured a hole in the boat
The Lord sent a hail storm against the boat
The Lord sent a great wind threatening the ship

question: What did the sailors do when they were afraid of the boat breaking up (Jn 1:5)?
options: They cried out to the Lord their God
They threw the cargo into the sea
They began throwing passengers into the sea
They took down the sail

question: What did Jonah do when the wind threatened to break up the boat (Jn 1:5)?
options: Helped the sailors throw the cargo overboard
Cried out to the gods of the sailors
Went below deck and fell asleep
Tore the sail down

question: What did the captain tell Jonah to do (Jn 1:6)?
options: Prepare to abandon ship
Prepare to be thrown overboard
Prepare to swim
Call on his god

question: What had Jonah already told the sailors (Jn 1:10)?
options: That he was under the curse of God
That he was a prophet of the Lord God Almighty
That he was running away from the Lord
That he was sent to Nineveh

question: What did Jonah tell the sailors to do in order to make the sea calm down (Jn 1:12)?
options: Throw him into the sea
Row harder for land
Throw the cargo overboard
Pray to the God of Israel

question: What did the sailors do after Jonah told them to throw him into the turbulent sea (Jn 1:13)?
options: They immediately threw him overboard
They tried to row back to land
They put a life preserver on him
They went behind an island to avoid the wind

question: After the sea grew even wilder what did the sailors do (Jn 1:14)?
options: They rowed even harder to get back to land
They cried out to the Lord
They started ripping up parts of the boat to lighten it
They were washed overboard

question: What was the response of the sea after the sailors threw Jonah overboard (Jn 1:15)?
options: The ship struck a sand bar
The ship pulled into a harbor and was spared
The boat ran aground on an island
The sea grew calm

question: When the sea grew calm what was the sailors response (Jn 1:16)?
options: They greatly feared the Lord
They praised the God of Israel
They looked for land
They raised the sail once again

question: After the sea grew calm the sailors did all of the following EXCEPT (Jn 1:16)
options: Feared the Lord greatly
Offered a sacrifice to the Lord
Worshipped the God of Israel
Made vows to the Lord

question: How long was Jonah in the belly of the fish (Jn 1:16)?
options: One day and one night
Two days
Three days and three nights
Seven days and seven nights

question: What did Jonah do from inside the fish (Jn 2:1)?
options: He slept
He prayed
He struggled
He passed out

question: From where did Jonah call for help (Jn 2:2)?
options: From the depths of the grave
From the bottom of the sea
From the stomach of a great fish
From the deep waters

question: What swept over Jonah (Jn 2:3)?
options: God’s waves and breakers
God’s swells of water
Aloneness in the deep
The waters of the Lord

question: In his prayer, from where did Jonah say he was banished (Jn 2:4)?
options: From the land of the living
From God’s sight
From the memory of the living
From his homeland

question: Where did Jonah say he would look again (Jn 2:4)?
options: Toward the sun
Toward heaven, God’s dwelling place
Toward God’s holy temple
Toward the holy land

question: What barred Jonah in forever (Jn 2:6)?
options: The grave
The earth
The mountains
The depths of the sea

question: From where did God bring up Jonah’s life (Jn 2:6)?
options: From the belly of the great fish
From the prison of the sea
From the pit
From the caves of the deep

question: What did Jonah say he did when his life was ebbing away (Jn 2:7)?
options: He cried out to the Lord
He remembered better days
He sought the Spirit of God
He remembered the Lord

question: Who forfeit the grace that could be theirs (Jn 2:8)?
options: Those who do not call out to the Lord
Those who cling to worthless idols
Those who cannot confess their own sins
Those who trust in themselves

question: What does Jonah in his prayer say comes from the Lord (Jn 2:9)?
options: Light
Holiness
Forgiveness
Salvation

question: What was the message the second time when the word of the Lord came to Jonah (Jn 3:1f)?
options: Repent and return to Nineveh where you will speak to the king
Go to the great city Nineveh and proclaim the message God will give
I am the God of all the earth therefore go to Nineveh and tell them to repent
Tell Nineveh to repent or in 60 days I will destroy the city

question: What message did Jonah preach in Nineveh upon entering the city (Jn 3:4)?
options: Forty more days and Nineveh will be overturned
Seven days and God will destroy the walls of Nineveh
In twenty days the Babylonians will destroy Nineveh
The gods of Nineveh will be burned with fire for seven days

question: What was Nineveh’s response to Jonah’s preaching (Jn 3:5)?
options: They tore their garments and put dust on their heads
They ordered all the gods of stone and silver to be destroyed
They humbled themselves and sought the Lord
They believed God and declared a fast and put on sackcloth

question: What did the king of Nineveh order in the decree after hearing Jonah’s message (Jn 3:7)?
options: They were to stop oppressing the poor and to seek justice
Both man and beast should not eat or drink anything
They were to cleanse their temples of statues of Marduk
They were to eat only bitter herbs for three days

question: What did the king of Nineveh order in the decree after hearing Jonah’s message (Jn 3:7)?
options: They were to stop oppressing the poor and to seek justice
They were to cleanse their temples of statues of Marduk
They were to eat only bitter herbs for three days
Both man and beast were to be covered with sackcloth

question: What did the king of Nineveh order in the decree after hearing Jonah’s message (Jn 3:7)?
options: They were to stop oppressing the poor and to seek justice
They should call on their gods pleading for mercy
They should urgently call upon God
They were to eat only bitter herbs for three days

question: What was the king of Nineveh hoping in his decree to his people (Jn 3:9)?
options: God would see their repentance and spare the city from fire
God would relent and turn from his fierce anger
God would send rain to relieve the drought
God would teach them his ways

question: What did God see after the king of Nineveh’s decree (Jn 3:10)?
options: Their fasting and humbling themselves before him
They truly were seeking the Lord
They freed the slaves and put down their weapons of war
How they turned from their evil ways

question: When Nineveh repented what was Jonah’s response (Jn 4:1)?
options: He was silent and refused to speak
He was displeased and angry
He was delighted they repented
He was disappointed and went away saddened

question: After God spared Nineveh what did Jonah wish for himself (Jn 4:3)?
options: That the Lord would take away his life
That the Lord would allow him to return to Israel
That he might return to the temple at Jerusalem
That he might see the devastation in Samaria by the Assyrians

question: Where did Jonah go after expressing his displeasure about the sparing of Nineveh (Jn 4:5)?
options: North of the city
South of the city
East of the city
West of the city

question: What did Jonah make for himself (Jn 4:5)?
options: A place to sit rest and wait and see what would happen
An altar to offer up sacrifices to the Lord
A cup so he could drink while he waited
Made a shelter to sit and wait in its shade

question: What was Jonah happy about (Jn 4:6)?
options: The vine God had made for him
The fire on the walls of Nineveh
The rain that refreshed him
That Nineveh listened to him and repented

question: After the vine grew up what did God make next (Jn 4:7)?
options: A bird that lodged in the vine
A worm which chewed the vine so it withered
A swarm of locusts which came and ate the vine
A snake which came and went up into the vine

question: Besides the blazing sun what did God send so that Jonah grew faint (Jn 4:8)?
options: A strong south wind
A dust storm
A scorching east wind
A messenger from Nineveh

question: What after the vine died what did God ask Jonah (Jn 4:9)?
options: Do you have a right to be angry about the vine?
What do you want to replace the vine with?
How long before you understand?
Why don’t you return to Israel?

question: The Lord noted what had Jonah showed concern for (Jn 4:10)?
options: The people of Israel over those of Assyria
The vine which grew up overnight and died overnight
The fatherless and widows in Nineveh
The worm that ate the vine
